title: network namespace is not shared in pod
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Network name space is not shared between containers in the same pod.
Two containers in same pod are able to start and listen on same port.
/etc/hosts doesn't have any entries about other containers in the pod.
This is happening only when `--network` option is used.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```bash
din@ /home/din $ podman run -dt --pod new:mypod --network=bridge -u 2000 --cap-drop ALL --cap-add NET_BIND_SERVICE --rm --name sylog-drop-2 47bcf5b7ece8
25fc2359ac27c5c2bc2ac5b83cecf6a4ff8dc8fd1fc631e2d4841a294aae9c34
din@ /home/din $ podman run -dt --pod mypod --network=bridge -u 1000 --cap-drop ALL --cap-add NET_BIND_SERVICE --rm --name sylog-drop 47bcf5b7ece8
bd97943074e8263b328705bb2813aa6ffaa42dc2bdee90481e6c1ce12f3c1351
din@ /home/din $ 
din@ /home/din $ podman ps -a --pod
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                         COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS             PORTS       NAMES               POD ID        PODNAME
9b37429654ab  registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/pause:latest                        25 seconds ago  Up 25 seconds ago              62a6e0daf2ff-infra  62a6e0daf2ff  mypod
25fc2359ac27  localhost/syslog:latest                       /usr/bin/start-rs...  25 seconds ago  Up 25 seconds ago              sylog-drop-2        62a6e0daf2ff  mypod
bd97943074e8  localhost/syslog:latest                       /usr/bin/start-rs...  6 seconds ago   Up 6 seconds ago               sylog-drop          62a6e0daf2ff  mypod
din@ /home/din $ podman exec -it sylog-drop ls -l /proc/1/ns
total 0
lrwxrwxrwx    1 1000     root             0 Feb 23 11:37 cgroup -> cgroup:[4026531835]
lrwxrwxrwx    1 1000     root             0 Feb 23 11:37 ipc -> ipc:[4026532484]
lrwxrwxrwx    1 1000     root             0 Feb 23 11:37 mnt -> mnt:[4026532554]
lrwxrwxrwx    1 1000     root             0 Feb 23 11:37 net -> net:[4026532489]
lrwxrwxrwx    1 1000     root             0 Feb 23 11:37 pid -> pid:[4026532557]
lrwxrwxrwx    1 1000     root             0 Feb 23 11:37 pid_for_children -> pid:[4026532557]
lrwxrwxrwx    1 1000     root             0 Feb 23 11:37 user -> user:[4026532269]
lrwxrwxrwx    1 1000     root             0 Feb 23 11:37 uts -> uts:[4026532483]
din@ /home/din $ podman exec -it sylog-drop-2 ls -l /proc/1/ns
total 0
lrwxrwxrwx    1 2000     root             0 Feb 23 11:37 cgroup -> cgroup:[4026531835]
lrwxrwxrwx    1 2000     root             0 Feb 23 11:37 ipc -> ipc:[4026532484]
lrwxrwxrwx    1 2000     root             0 Feb 23 11:37 mnt -> mnt:[4026532486]
lrwxrwxrwx    1 2000     root             0 Feb 23 11:37 net -> net:[4026532272]
lrwxrwxrwx    1 2000     root             0 Feb 23 11:37 pid -> pid:[4026532487]
lrwxrwxrwx    1 2000     root             0 Feb 23 11:37 pid_for_children -> pid:[4026532487]
lrwxrwxrwx    1 2000     root             0 Feb 23 11:37 user -> user:[4026532269]
lrwxrwxrwx    1 2000     root             0 Feb 23 11:37 uts -> uts:[4026532483]
din@ /home/din $ podman exec -it sylog-drop-2 cat /etc/hosts
127.0.0.1   localhost localhost.localdomain localhost4 localhost4.localdomain4
::1         localhost localhost.localdomain localhost6 localhost6.localdomain6

192.168.0.6  dink-ai.novalocal dink-ai
10.88.0.2       mypod 62a6e0daf2ff-infra
10.88.0.1 host.containers.internal
127.0.0.1 mypod sylog-drop-2
din@ /home/din $ podman exec -it sylog-drop cat /etc/hosts
127.0.0.1   localhost localhost.localdomain localhost4 localhost4.localdomain4
::1         localhost localhost.localdomain localhost6 localhost6.localdomain6

192.168.0.6  dink-ai.novalocal dink-ai
10.88.0.3       mypod sylog-drop
10.88.0.1 host.containers.internal
din@ /home/din $ 
din@ /home/din $ 
din@ /home/din $ podman exec -it 9b37429654ab cat /etc/hosts
Error: exec failed: container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: exec: "cat": executable file not found in $PATH: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
din@ /home/din $ 
din@ /home/din $ podman exec -it sylog-drop-2 netstat -nlap
Active Internet connections (servers and established)
Proto Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address           Foreign Address         State       PID/Program name    
tcp        0      0 0.0.0.0:601             0.0.0.0:*               LISTEN      1/rsyslogd
tcp        0      0 :::601                  :::*                    LISTEN      1/rsyslogd
Active UNIX domain sockets (servers and established)
Proto RefCnt Flags       Type       State         I-Node PID/Program name    Path
din@ /home/din $ podman exec -it sylog-drop netstat -nlap
Active Internet connections (servers and established)
Proto Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address           Foreign Address         State       PID/Program name    
tcp        0      0 0.0.0.0:601             0.0.0.0:*               LISTEN      1/rsyslogd
tcp        0      0 :::601                  :::*                    LISTEN      1/rsyslogd
Active UNIX domain sockets (servers and established)
Proto RefCnt Flags       Type       State         I-Node PID/Program name    Path
```


**Describe the results you expected:**
/etc/hosts having the details about other container in the same pod
Should be able to communicate with other container in pod using localhost
Should not be allowed to use same port within pod by different containers

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
din@ /home/din $ podman version
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.7
Built:        Wed Nov 10 02:53:56 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
din@ /home/din $ rpm -q podman
podman-3.3.1-9.module_el8.5.0+988+b1f0b741.x86_64
```

comments.body: Does it work when you create the pod with podman pod create?

Ah I see your problem you cannot use --network again for the other container. This will overwrite the pod namespace, see https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-run.1.md#--networkmode---net
Aah! yes. got it. thank you.
